text:
In a distant galaxy somewhere between Orion's arms and the Serubean clouds, there was a planet called Serenia, famous for its towering crystal forests and singing stones. Among its many legends was that of the Whispering Sunflower, a plant that could sing melodies of sunlight and grace.

There lived a humble shepherd named Aelius. Every morning, he tended to his flock near the edge of a clearing that hosted these extraordinary flowering vines. The sunflower vines had blossoms that seemed to capture the hues of the dawn, their surfaces covered in tiny, iridescent petals.

One day, while Aelius was tending his sheep, he noticed something peculiar. A petal appeared to sing, each note carrying the warmth of the morning sun.

Curious, Aelius approached. The petals didn't move, but they sang louder. The singing grew into a melody, gentle and welcoming.

"Why don't you join us?" Aelius asked the petals.

They hummed.
